CAT02-082.headline: Exposure And Response Prevention
CAT02-082.subtitle: facing fear without giving in to the urge
CAT02-082.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans slightly toward an open space with one hand outstretched yet steady, posture brave and willing, conveying the quiet courage of approaching what unsettles rather than fleeing it.
CAT02-082.sceneLine: A loose ring of plum accent forms hovers just beyond the figure's reach without ever closing, dissolving softly into pale plum at its edges, suggesting an urge that loses its grip when calmly faced.

CAT08-036.headline: Body-Focused Repetitive Behaviors
CAT08-036.subtitle: hair pulling, skin picking, and nail biting
CAT08-036.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests both hands open and still upon its knees, posture gentle and self-accepting, conveying the soft pause that interrupts an automatic habit.
CAT08-036.sceneLine: A small cluster of soft light plum threads loosens and settles loosely beside the figure rather than tightening, fading to pale plum, suggesting tension released instead of redirected onto the self.

CAT13-031.headline: OCD Explained
CAT13-031.subtitle: obsessions, compulsions, and effective treatment
CAT13-031.figureLine: A single seated clay figure sits upright with hands lightly clasped, head tilted in calm attentiveness, posture composed and curious, conveying steady understanding of an unsettling pattern.
CAT13-031.sceneLine: A looping plum accent thread circles once near the figure before opening into a soft pale plum curve, suggesting a repeating cycle that can finally be eased open.

CAT13-032.headline: OCD Subtypes
CAT13-032.subtitle: contamination, harm, symmetry, and pure O
CAT13-032.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands centered and balanced with arms held in calm symmetry, posture even and grounded, conveying steadiness amid many different pulls.
CAT13-032.sceneLine: Four distinct soft light plum forms of varying shape rest in gentle balance around the figure, each settling into the same pale plum stillness, suggesting different patterns sharing one calm center.

CAT13-033.headline: Body Dysmorphic Disorder
CAT13-033.subtitle: symptoms, risks, and treatment
CAT13-033.figureLine: A single standing clay figure turns gently toward soft light with one hand resting kindly over its own chest, posture self-compassionate and at ease, conveying a kinder way of seeing oneself.
CAT13-033.sceneLine: A wavering plum accent reflection beside the figure slowly steadies into a clear, gentle pale plum form, suggesting a distorted self-image quietly coming into truer focus.

CAT13-034.headline: Trichotillomania
CAT13-034.subtitle: understanding compulsive hair pulling
CAT13-034.figureLine: A single seated clay figure folds both hands together softly in its lap, shoulders lowered and calm, posture patient and self-kind, conveying a gentle pause held with care.
CAT13-034.sceneLine: A few loose soft light plum strands drift slowly downward and come to rest untouched beside the figure, dissolving into pale plum, suggesting an urge allowed to settle on its own.

CAT13-035.headline: Excoriation Disorder
CAT13-035.subtitle: understanding skin-picking and treatment
CAT13-035.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests both open palms gently upturned and still, posture soft and forgiving, conveying tender self-restraint and care toward the body.
CAT13-035.sceneLine: A small plum accent ripple beside the figure smooths outward into an unbroken pale plum surface, suggesting roughness gently soothed rather than worried.

CAT13-037.headline: Hoarding Disorder
CAT13-037.subtitle: prevalence, causes, and treatment
CAT13-037.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands within newly opened space, arms relaxed at its sides, posture unburdened and breathing room around it, conveying the relief of gentle release.
CAT13-037.sceneLine: A dense gathering of soft light plum forms thins gradually outward into clear pale plum openness beside the figure, suggesting accumulated weight slowly making room for air.

CAT13-038.headline: The OCD Spectrum
CAT13-038.subtitle: related disorders and shared features
CAT13-038.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands calmly at the center with arms gently extended outward, posture connected and composed, conveying many related patterns held in one understanding.
CAT13-038.sceneLine: Several soft light plum forms of differing shapes drift around the figure yet trace back to one shared pale plum thread, suggesting distinct conditions linked by a common root.

CAT13-040.headline: Gold-Standard ERP Treatment
CAT13-040.subtitle: the leading therapy for OCD
CAT13-040.figureLine: A single standing clay figure steps forward with quiet resolve, one foot ahead and shoulders settled, posture confident and unhurried, conveying trust in a proven, steady path.
CAT13-040.sceneLine: A clear plum accent path unfolds ahead of the figure, each segment brightening gently into pale plum, suggesting a reliable route forward through what once felt impossible.
